chief_complaint: A 77-year-old woman presented with diplopia following a ground-level fall.
history: The patient initially had a National Institutes of Health Stroke Scale (NIHSS) score of 0 but later developed acute neurological symptoms with an NIHSS of 25. She was found to have a large posterior circulation ischaemic penumbra on CT angiography.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: CT angiography: large posterior circulation ischaemic penumbra, terminal basilar artery supplied primarily by a stenosed left Saltzman type I PTA.
correct_diagnosis: Persistent Trigeminal Artery (PTA) causing acute ischaemic stroke
differentials: Persistent Trigeminal Artery (PTA) causing acute ischaemic stroke
correct_ranking: Persistent Trigeminal Artery (PTA) causing acute ischaemic stroke
